overview: The Markdown Filter extracts translatable text from Markdown files based on the [CommonMark](http://commonmark.org) specification, with additional support for [GitHub-flavored Markdown](https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/). HTML inline elements and HTML blocks embedded in the Markdown are processed by a configurable HTML sub-filter. The filter also supports YAML front matter extraction via a configurable YAML sub-filter.

extraction.translateUrls.help:
URLs in link and image statements (e.g. `[text](url)`, `![alt](url)`) are extracted as a **subflow** — a separate translation unit that appears before the segment containing it.

Use with `urlPattern` to restrict which URLs are exposed for translation.

extraction.urlPattern.help:
Only URLs matching this regex will be extracted for translation. Only effective when `translateUrls` is enabled.

The pattern is matched against the full URL string.
extraction.urlPattern.values: Java regex pattern. Default `.+` matches all URLs.
examples: `^images/.*` — only translate URLs under the `images/` path

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this pattern to take effect

extraction.translateCodeBlocks.help: Controls whether content inside fenced code blocks (delimited by ``` or ~~~) is exposed for translation. Disable for source code that should remain untranslated.

extraction.translateIndentedCodeBlocks.help: Controls whether content in indented code blocks (lines indented by 4+ spaces or a tab) is exposed for translation.

extraction.translateInlineCodeBlocks.help: Controls whether text delimited by single backticks (`` `inline code` ``) is exposed for translation.

extraction.translateHeaderMetadata.help:
Parses [YAML metadata headers](http://pandoc.org/MANUAL.html#extension-yaml_metadata_block) (front matter delimited by `---`) and exposes metadata values for translation.

Use the `yamlSubfilter` parameter to customize which metadata fields are extracted.

extraction.translateImageAltText.help: Extracts alt text from both Markdown image syntax (`![alt text](url)`) and HTML `` tags (``) for translation.

extraction.nonTranslateBlocks.help: Prevents specific block quotes from being extracted for translation. Block quotes whose content starts with one of the specified strings will be skipped.
extraction.nonTranslateBlocks.values: Comma-separated list of string prefixes. Empty means all block quotes are extracted.

extraction.parseMdx.help: **Experimental.** Parses out multi-line `export` blocks as skeleton (non-translatable structure), allowing MDX files to be processed without breaking on JSX-like syntax.
notes: This feature is experimental and uses regex-based parsing of MDX expressions.

output.generateHeaderAnchors.help: Automatically generates explicit named anchors for headings using `{#anchor-id}` syntax. This provides stable anchor targets for hyperlinks that reference translatable header values — without this, translated headers would break anchor links.

output.charactersToEscape.help: Characters listed here will be backslash-escaped on export. Only takes effect when `unescapeBackslashCharacters` is enabled.
output.charactersToEscape.values: A string of individual punctuation characters (not separated by delimiters). Default: ``*_`{}[]<>()#+\-.!|``

dependsOn.condition: must be true for escaping to be applied on export

output.htmlEntitiesToEscape.help: Encodes specific characters as HTML entities on export. Useful when the target system requires certain characters to be entity-encoded.
output.htmlEntitiesToEscape.values: A string of individual characters to encode as HTML entities.

output.unescapeBackslashCharacters.help: Parses backslash-escaped punctuation in source documents (e.g., `\*` is read as a literal `*`). On export, the characters listed in `charactersToEscape` are re-escaped with backslashes.

subfilters.htmlSubfilter.help:
Specifies a custom HTML filter configuration for processing HTML content within Markdown. The configuration file must be saved with a `.fprm` suffix in a known location.

Leave empty to use the default HTML filter configuration tailored for Markdown.

To use inline code patterns for HTML content, configure them in the HTML filter separately and reference it here.
subfilters.htmlSubfilter.values: A configuration ID in the form `okf_html@arbitrary-name` (without the `.fprm` suffix).
notes: The Inline Code Finder configured on the Markdown filter applies only to Markdown text, not to HTML inline tags or HTML blocks. For HTML content, configure inline codes in the HTML sub-filter separately.

subfilters.yamlSubfilter.help:
Specifies a custom YAML filter configuration for processing YAML metadata headers (front matter). This allows customization of which metadata fields are extracted for translation.

Leave empty to use the default YAML filter configuration.
subfilters.yamlSubfilter.values: A YAML filter configuration ID (without the `.fprm` suffix).

inlineCodes.help:
The [Inline Code Finder](HTML_Filter#Inline_Code_Finder) lets you define regex patterns that identify inline codes within translatable text. These codes are treated as placeholders during translation.

The inline code patterns apply only to Markdown text content — they do **not** apply to HTML inline tags or HTML blocks within the document.
notes: Inline Code Finder support was temporarily unavailable in some snapshot builds of version 0.36, but has been restored.

processingNotes: If the file has a Unicode Byte-Order-Mark (BOM), the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used automatically. Otherwise, the default encoding specified in the filter options is used.
HTML inline elements and HTML blocks embedded in Markdown are delegated to the HTML filter for processing. The HTML filter can be customized via the `htmlSubfilter` parameter.
URLs extracted for translation appear as **subflows** — separate translation units that are extracted before the segment containing them.

examples.title: URL translation as subflows
examples.description: When `translateUrls` is enabled, URLs in image/link syntax are extracted as separate translation units (subflows) that appear before the containing segment in the output.
examples.input:
```markdown
Please click ![The Information desk logo](images/circled-i.jpg) for help.
```
examples.output:
```xml
<trans-unit id="tu2" restype="x-img-link" xml:space="preserve">
  <source xml:lang="en">images/circled-i.jpg</source>
</trans-unit>
<trans-unit id="tu1" xml:space="preserve">
  <source xml:lang="en">Please click <bpt id="1">![</bpt>The Information desk logo<ept id="1">]([#$tu2])</ept> for help.</source>
</trans-unit>
```
